paper_title: Laboratory and Field Measurement of Sediment Resistivity in Lake Nahá

methods.description: This method involves determining the electrical resistivity of sediment samples in the laboratory to use as a constraint during inversion processes. The resistivity measurements help in understanding the sediment composition and variations.

steps.description: The 49 cm-long core Nah-16 I was recovered in June 2016 from the deepest part of Lake Nahá.
steps.searchable_summary: recover sediment core from Lake Nahá

steps.description: The core was sampled every 0.5 cm, packed into small sealed plastic bags, and kept cool during transport and storage.
steps.searchable_summary: sample and store sediment core at 0.5 cm intervals

steps.description: The core material was filled into a four-point measuring cell with a diameter of 2.5 cm and a length of 4.5 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: fill measuring cell with sediment samples

steps.description: Resistivity was measured using a Chameleon I electrical-impedance analyzer at different frequencies between 1 mHz and 1 kHz.
The filled sample holder was stored in a climate chamber at 20°C for at least 24 hours before measurement.
steps.searchable_summary: store sample holder in climate chamber

methods.method_name: Laboratory Measurements Narrow Down Sediment Resistivity
methods.description: This method refines the resistivity values of sediment samples by analyzing variations due to packing procedures and core depth, providing a range for inversion of field data.

steps.description: Analyze resistivity variation with packing procedure and core depth.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze resistivity variation by packing and depth

steps.description: Use a broad range of 8.0-12.0 Ωm as the upper and lower limit for inversion of field data.
steps.searchable_summary: set resistivity range for field data inversion
